differences.title: 1. FOCUS
differences.leftDesc: Specifies the desired result without explicitly stating control flow.
differences.rightDesc: Specifies a sequence of commands to change program state.

differences.title: 2. READABILITY
differences.leftDesc: Often more concise and easier to understand the intent.
differences.rightDesc: Can be verbose, requiring careful reading of each step.

differences.title: 3. FLEXIBILITY
differences.leftDesc: Less control over execution path, harder to tweak details.
differences.rightDesc: Full control over execution, allows fine-grained adjustments.

differences.title: 4. STATE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: State changes are often managed implicitly by the system.
differences.rightDesc: State mutations are explicitly coded and managed.

differences.title: 5. COMPLEXITY
differences.leftDesc: Can simplify complex problems by abstracting away details.
differences.rightDesc: Requires careful management of variables and control flow.

differences.title: 6. SIDE EFFECTS
differences.leftDesc: Aims to reduce or eliminate side effects for predictability.
differences.rightDesc: Side effects are common and explicitly handled.

differences.title: 7. COMMON EXAMPLES
differences.leftDesc: Focuses on describing the final structure and behavior.
differences.rightDesc: Focuses on the sequence of operations to achieve a goal.
